notes: YELLOW/GREEN - A few spotty downpours are possible. Coverage is maybe only 20% or so, but a delay is still possible if you get the wrong storm in the wrong spot at the wrong time. Warm with winds blowing out, bump to bats.
GREEN/YELLOW - Looks to me like any rain is clear prior to first pitch. I'll confirm that with a pregame radar check, but I'm not expecting any trouble here.

YELLOW/ORANGE - Should start dry, then a cluster of showers and storms moves in towards the ends of the game. I'm hopeful that it comes after the game, but if it doesn't it could be a long wait to finish 9 (or maybe even a game that doesn't go a full 9).

YELLOW - This actually looks a lot like the PHI game, in that it starts dry and likely plays the entire game juuust before a line of storms moves in. I won't rule out some late inning trouble, and if it happens it could mean game-over... but I think it's more likely they finish 9 before the storms show up.

A few spotty downpours are possible. Coverage is maybe only 20% or so, but a delay is still possible if you get the wrong storm in the wrong spot at the wrong time. Warm with winds blowing out, bump to bats.

YELLOW - Another game with some later-inning drama possible. Most of the game plays dry, but the later innings could see some showers and storms. Warm with winds blowing out to center at 10mph, boost to bats.

YELLOW - This is another 'maybe overly cautious YELLOW tag'. There's a few storms around late... but the likely outcome remains a dry 9 before the rain is a worry. Still, later-inning trouble is certainly possible, hence the tag.
Warm with winds blowing out to left at 10-15mph, solid hitting weather.
